items.title: Koopje van 75 miljoen pond: toen Virgil van Dijk de beste winterdeal ooit werd
items.summary: ‘Hij is een goede speler, maar 75 miljoen pond? Nee, dat is hij zeker niet waard.’ Alan Shearer trok er het zuinige gezicht bij dat hij zo vaak trekt wanneer iemand naar zijn mening vraagt, in dit geval door de BBC. ‘ A hell of a deal voor Southampton ’, voegde de voormalige spits van Southampton er ook nog aan toe. Acht jaar later bekeken is de overstap van Virgil van Dijk naar Liverpool misschien wel de beste winterdeal aller tijden.

items.title: Boel reageert op kritiek: 'In augustus hadden we hiervoor getekend'
items.summary: De kritiek op Go Ahead Eagles-trainer Melvin Boel neemt toe, maar de vraag is of dat fair is. Ja, Go Ahead wint te weinig wedstrijden en speelt niet meer zo flitsend als vorig seizoen. Feit is ook dat vorig seizoen een droomjaar was en dat de club al jaren in de lift zit.

items.title: Ranked: The top 25 manager jobs in soccer, from best to worst
items.summary: Most of soccer's top coaching jobs will be vacant in the next year or so. Let's rank which jobs are the most -- and least -- attractive, from clubs to national teams.

items.title: Predicting what happens to Europe's top managers this summer
items.summary: One thing is for certain this summer: The world's best managers will be in high demand.

items.title: Slot: 'Liverpool unlucky?' after Jacquet injury
items.summary: Arne Slot has said Liverpool's luck this season has been so bad it has even extended to new signing Jérémy Jacquet, who has suffered a shoulder injury.

items.title: Berbatov: 'Wait and see' on Carrick as manager
items.summary: Former Manchester United striker Dimitar Berbatov has hailed the turnaround at Old Trafford under Michael Carrick but urged the club not to rush a decision on a permanent managerial role.

items.title: Bayern: Kane in 'intensive talks' over future
items.summary: Bayern Munich remain in "intensive talks" with Harry Kane over his future, according to the club's sporting director Max Eberl.

items.title: Cruijff ontslaat beloftentrainer Weijs: 'Er is vernieuwing nodig bij Jong Ajax'
items.summary: Willem Weijs dient zijn contract als trainer van Jong Ajax niet uit. De Amsterdammers nemen per direct afscheid van de oefenmeester.

items.title: Kroos haalt weer uit naar Saudi Pro League: 'Zonder Ronaldo zou niemand kijken'
items.summary: Toni Kroos heeft andermaal laten blijken geen fan te zijn van de Saudi Pro League. De ontwikkelingen rondom Cristiano Ronaldo geven de voormalige middenvelder daartoe aanleiding in zijn podcast Einfach mal Luppen .

items.title: Has Gyokeres finally found form or are stats misleading?
items.summary: Arsenal are top of the league, fighting for trophies in four competitions and at the same time £64m striker Viktor Gyokeres looks to be finding his feet in north London.

items.title: Joeri de Kamps duikt na acht maanden zonder club op in Roemenië
items.summary: Joeri de Kamps (34) heeft na ruim een halfjaar een nieuwe club gevonden. De middenvelder vervolgt zijn carrière in Roemenië. Bij ACS Sepsi OSK Sfântu Gheorghe om precies te zijn.

items.title: Slot admits missing out on Champions League ‘not acceptable’ for Liverpool
items.summary: Manager says this season has been toughest of career Liverpool lack right-back options for Sunderland game Arne Slot has said it would be unacceptable for Liverpool not to qualify for the Champions League and that this season has been the toughest of his managerial career “by a mile”. The Premier League champions lie sixth, five points off fourth place, although fifth is likely to yield a Champions League spot. Liverpool, who spent almost £450m on players last summer, are heavily reliant on Champions League income for their business model but have won once in seven league games before Wednesday’s visit to Sunderland. Continue reading...

items.title: Chelsea VAR penalty at Crystal Palace a mistake, panel says
items.summary: Chelsea should not have been awarded a penalty in their 3-1 win over Crystal Palace last month, when Joao Pedro's goal-bound shot was blocked by Jaydee Canvot’s arm.

items.title: Toughest season I've had as manager 'by a mile' - Slot
items.summary: Liverpool boss Arne Slot says this season is the toughest he has had as a manager "by a mile" and his side are "not performing to Liverpool's standards".

items.title: Lindsey Heaps: ‘The Champions League is the baby you always want to win’
items.summary: US captain reflects on her playing career in France and the need for greater competition as she prepares for a summer move to Denver Lindsey Heaps is sitting in the heart of Lyon, a city that has witnessed her transformation from a self-described “baby” into the authoritative captain of the US women’s national team. Now wearing the iconic No 10 shirt for OL Lyonnes, inherited this season from Dzsenifer Marozsán, Heaps is reflective. She is a veteran, a leader who has won almost everything, yet she remains a student of the game, constantly seeking the “good struggles” that defined her early years. The timing of our meeting is poignant. This month Lyonnes reasserted their dominance over the Première Ligue with a 1-0 victory against Paris Saint-Germain, before winning 4-0 against Saint-Étienne in a derby. The results leaves OL in a league of their own: 14 points clear of second-placed Nantes, with PSG cast adrift in fifth place, 17 points behind the leaders. For Heaps, these numbers are not just a source of pride; they are a symptom of a wider problem. Continue reading...

items.title: David Squires on … the chaos at Anfield as Manchester City stay in title chase
items.summary: Our cartoonist looks back at the mayhem on Merseyside as visitors’ late win reminded Arsenal they’re still in the hunt Buy a cartoon | David’s favourite work of 2025 And his latest book, Chaos in the Box: get it now Continue reading...

items.title: Lyon loan helping 'explosive' Endrick's World Cup 'dream' with Brazil
items.summary: Real Madrid striker Endrick's impact on loan at Lyon has been immediate, and is fuelling the teenager's "dream" of playing for Brazil at the World Cup.

items.title: Infantino dogged by threat of Russia and fear of Trump as he heads to sweet-talk Uefa
items.summary: Fifa president will be under major scrutiny when he goes to Brussels to address the Uefa annual congress on Thursday Assuming Gianni Infantino turns up on time, he is expected to make his customary address to Uefa’s annual congress on Thursday. The couple of hours spent in Brussels Expo Hall 3 will be largely procedural but the Fifa president’s messaging will be worth delegates’ attention. Even by the standards of relations between football’s major governing bodies, the past 12 months have been fractious. The fault lines hardly get narrower and there is certainly no reduction in the number of thorny issues simmering away. At last year’s edition, in Belgrade, Infantino used the gathering of European football’s great and good to make a caveated case for Russia’s return to competitive action. If that was a rolling of the pitch, his comments on the matter in an interview last week amounted to letting the sprinklers loose. Infantino said the ban on Russian sides should be reassessed, at least for age-group teams, but there is little chance of his views gaining weight around Europe even if he elects to revisit the argument. Continue reading...

items.title: Le Bris analyses the key players in a Sunderland team defying history
items.summary: It's February - and Sunderland are four points away from reaching 40 points. Premier League safety would be secured with a few months to spare - in a sensational case of rewriting history.
